summary: Reporter deploys a MAUI Windows app using LiveCharts2 and SkiaSharp 3.119.1, then sees a startup crash on some Windows 11 systems with COMException ('Element not found') and TypeInitializationException from SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.PropertySetExtensions. The stack trace points to the WinUI swapchain rendering path during AngleSwapChainPanel initialization. The issue is Windows-specific and currently open with only partial reproduction detail.

stepsToReproduce: Deploy a MAUI app on Windows that uses the WinUI swapchain rendering path through SkiaSharp.
Open a page that triggers WinUI swapchain surface creation for chart rendering.
Observe TypeInitializationException from WinUI native PropertySet extensions on affected machines.
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Windows 11 (10.0.26100.7462), Lenovo 83C4, SkiaSharp 3.119.1, MAUI app with LiveCharts2

bugSignals.errorMessage: System.TypeInitializationException for SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.PropertySetExtensions with inner COMException: Element not found.
bugSignals.stackTrace: at BaseActivationFactory(string typeNamespace, string typeFullName) -> PropertySetExtensions.AddSingle(...) -> GlesContext.CreateSurface(...) -> AngleSwapChainPanel.EnsureRenderSurface() -> AngleSwapChainPanel.OnLoaded(...)

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: Current WinUI surface creation code still routes through PropertySetExtensions activation in GlesContext without fallback, so the reported failure path remains active in current source.

analysis.summary: The failure happens before drawing, while initializing the WinUI swapchain surface: GlesContext creates a PropertySet and calls WinUI native extension methods to insert size/scale values, and the generated WinRT activation for that extension type fails on affected systems. The source path confirms this is tightly coupled to the WinUI ANGLE/OpenGL swapchain path, not the CPU canvas path. Related historical issues show similar activation failures (class registration/activation errors) in the same component family, often tied to packaging/runtime environment differences.
analysis.rationale: This is a bug because expected rendering startup throws a runtime exception and aborts the rendering path in normal app usage. The stack trace and source citations localize the fault to the Windows/WinUI swapchain implementation used by MAUI on Windows, so area/SkiaSharp.Views.Maui with Windows-WinUI and OpenGL backend labels best capture impact surface. Severity is high because rendering is blocked by startup-time exception, but root cause still needs targeted repro matrix confirmation (packaging mode, RID/assets, machine-specific runtime state).

keySignals.text: System.TypeInitializationException: The type initializer for 'SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.PropertySetExtensions' threw an exception.
keySignals.interpretation: Failure is in WinRT activation for WinUI native helper type, before drawing operations proceed.
Crash is in WinUI swapchain surface initialization path, specifically ANGLE property-set population.

keySignals.text: Issue #2968 and #3233 report the same WinUI swapchain family failing in Windows MAUI scenarios.
keySignals.interpretation: There is prior evidence of persistent Windows swapchain/activation instability, so this is likely related rather than isolated user error.

codeInvestigation.finding: CreateSurface builds a PropertySet and calls PropertySetExtensions.AddSize/AddSingle before creating EGL window surface; this matches the reported failing stack location.
EnsureRenderSurface unconditionally calls glesContext.CreateSurface during load/size lifecycle when surface is missing, consistent with startup crash timing.
PropertySetExtensions is a WinRT runtimeclass; activation must succeed at runtime for AddSingle/AddSize to be callable.
Native implementation only wraps PropertyValue inserts, indicating failure is likely activation/deployment/runtime context before method body execution.
CPU canvas rendering path paints via SKSurface over WriteableBitmap and does not depend on the WinUI PropertySetExtensions activation path.
Docs indicate WinUI view package depends on WinUI native assets and ANGLE libraries, reinforcing that deployment/runtime asset state is part of diagnosis.

workarounds: Use the CPU-backed Windows rendering path instead of the GPU swapchain path on affected systems to bypass WinUI native activation in this code path.
Validate Windows packaging/deployment mode and ensure WinUI native assets are present for the exact target RID in the app output.
nextQuestions: Does the failure reproduce in both packaged (MSIX) and unpackaged deployment modes?
Which exact Windows target framework and RID are used in the app project at publish time?
Does switching to the CPU rendering path avoid the crash on affected machines?
Are SkiaSharp.Views.WinUI.Native.dll, libEGL.dll, and libGLESv2.dll present in the final deployed output on failing machines?

resolution.hypothesis: A WinRT activation/deployment mismatch in the WinUI swapchain initialization path causes PropertySetExtensions activation to fail on certain Windows environments.

proposals.title: Use CPU rendering path on affected Windows targets
proposals.description: Temporarily use the non-swapchain rendering route for Windows deployments impacted by this startup failure.

proposals.title: Verify WinUI native asset deployment for target RID
proposals.description: Confirm the app project directly deploys WinUI native assets and that deployed output includes required WinUI native and ANGLE files for the target architecture.

proposals.title: Run packaging matrix repro to isolate activation conditions
proposals.description: Compare packaged and unpackaged Windows deployments with the same app to isolate whether activation failure is environment-specific or generally reproducible.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Use CPU rendering path on affected Windows targets
resolution.recommendedReason: It is the fastest low-risk mitigation for users while maintainers investigate WinUI swapchain activation and deployment conditions.

actionability.reason: Consistent crash evidence exists in active WinUI swapchain startup path, but root activation/deployment trigger needs focused reproduction on affected systems.

missingInfo: Whether deployment is packaged (MSIX) or unpackaged.
Target framework and RID used in the failing publish/build.
Whether CPU rendering path avoids the crash on the same machine.
File list from deployed output showing WinUI native and ANGLE binaries.

actions.description: Ensure labels include bug, Windows WinUI platform, OpenGL backend, and MAUI partner context.
Link to active related Windows MAUI swapchain crash thread.
Link to historical WinUI unpackaged swapchain failure discussion for context.
